articles.title: Union Minister for Finance & Corporate Affairs Smt. Nirmala Sitharaman participates in virtual meeting on Global Convergence for Growth Summit, from New Delhi, today
articles.full_content:
Consequences of conflicts disproportionately fall on developing countries and the Global South, and this situation demands coordinated global action: FM Smt. Sitharaman “Recent developments highlight importance of resilient, diversified and geographically distributed supply chains”
Union Minister for Finance & Corporate Affairs Smt. Nirmala Sitharaman participated in the virtual meeting on Global Convergence for Growth Summit, from New Delhi, today.
The Summit was held to bring together leaders of advanced and emerging economies to discuss ways of supporting a balanced growth anchored in an efficient global framework. French President H.E. Mr. Emmanuel Macron presided over the Summit that witnessed participation of the top leadership from all the G7 nations, India, Brazil, China, Kenya, South Korea and IMF.
During her intervention at the Summit representing India, FM Smt. Sitharaman said, “In today’s interconnected world, prosperity and challenges are shared, but the consequences of conflicts and uncertainty fall disproportionately on developing countries and the Global South. The situation demands coordinated global action. We must strengthen multilateral cooperation to build resilient economies, accelerate sustainable development, and ensure inclusive growth that benefits all.”
On the issue of global imbalances, the Union Finance Minister said, “Not all imbalances are alike, some reflect differences in demographics, development stages, resource endowments, or economic structures. Our focus should, therefore, remain on excessive and persistent imbalances while recognising that the scale of domestic needs varies significantly across countries.”
Speaking for the Global South, Smt. Sitharaman further said, “The burden of adjustment should not fall disproportionately on countries that are not the drivers of these imbalances. India, like many developing economies, remains largely peripheral to both the origination and propagation of global imbalances; yet, we continue to face their spillover effects.”
Highlighting India's remarkable economic progress in recent years through the visionary leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, and India’s commitment to the mantra of Reform, Perform, and Transform, the Union Finance Minister said, “Our growth is primarily domestic-demand led, with a largely market-determined exchange rate. India continues to be among the world’s fastest-growing major economies, with GDP growth projected to remain robust at around 7 percent over the medium term.”
FM Smt. Sitharaman emphasised the need to strengthen confidence in multilateral institutions and ensure that they remain responsive to the needs of developing countries. She called for better, bigger, more effective and more representative Multilateral Development Banks that can deliver significantly greater financing to developing countries and emerging economies, noting that enhancing their financing capacity, operational agility and responsiveness will be critical.
articles.summary: Sitharaman “Recent developments highlight importance of resilient, diversified and geographically distributed supply chains” Union Minister for Finance & Corporate Affairs Smt. Nirmala Sitharaman participated in the virtual meeting on Global Convergence for Growth Summit, from New Delhi, today. The Summit was held to bring together leaders of advanced and emerging economies to discuss ways of supporting a balanced growth anchored in an efficient global framework.

ICMR-NIOHR Pays Homage to Flight AI171 Victims
articles.full_content:
The ICMR National Institute of Occupational Health Research (ICMR-NIOHR), Ahmedabad, observed the first remembrance today, June 11, 2026, of the tragic Air India Flight AI171 crash that occurred right in front of the institute on June 12, 2025.
This event was dedicated to paying heartfelt tribute to all those who lost their lives in this tragic accident including all passengers and crew members on board, medical students and doctors of the Civil Hospital along with their family members, passersby, and other local people on the ground and to honouring the institute's Karmyogis who played a crucial role in emergency relief operations. The Karmyogis of the institute remembered this horrific tragedy by observing a collective silence and offering prayers. On this occasion, a special condolence message was also presented on behalf of all the officers and staff members.
Along with the tribute, letters of honour were presented to the Karmyogis of NIOHR who acted as 'first responders' immediately after the accident, recognizing their exceptional selflessness, and courage. Their prompt efforts to assist the emergency officials engaged in rescue and relief operations went far beyond the normal expectations of duty. Additionally, a comprehensive firefighting and emergency safety training session was organized for the Karmyogis, aimed at strengthening workplace safety, enhancing institutional preparedness, and building rapid response capabilities for any future emergencies.
Through this entire event, while paying humble tribute to the deceased, ICMR-NIOHR has further reinforced its commitment to workplace safety, institutional crisis resilience, and the selfless public service of its Karmyogis.
